skill.name: google-agents-cli-adk-code
skill.description: Quick reference for Google ADK Python API patterns: agent definitions, tools, callbacks, multi-agent orchestration, state management, and graph workflows.
skill.summary: Quick reference for Google ADK Python API patterns: agent definitions, tools, callbacks, multi-agent orchestration, state management, and graph workflows.
skill.author: google

security_audit.summary: This skill consists of 3 Markdown documentation files (SKILL.md, references/adk-python.md, references/adk-workflows.md) containing API reference material for Google ADK. The 478 static findings are overwhelmingly false positives: backtick matches are Python code examples in markdown blocks, URL matches are legitimate documentation links to adk.dev, and env_access refers to a .env file mention in a project layout diagram. No prompt injection, no malicious intent, no executable payload.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks matched as shell backticks
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged backticks throughout markdown files as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are legitimate Markdown code block delimiters wrapping Python examples (e.g., `Agent(...)`, `tools=[get_weather]`). No code execution risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged URLs pointing to adk.dev documentation pages (e.g., https://adk.dev/llms.txt, https://adk.dev/workflows/index.md). These are legitimate reference links in a documentation skill, not exfiltration endpoints.

low_findings.title: .env file referenced in project layout diagram
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged .env mention in the standard ADK project structure diagram. This is documentation showing where environment files belong in an ADK project, not actual file access.

content.user_title: Write Google ADK Agent Code with Confidence
content.value_statement: Building agents with Google ADK requires knowing dozens of API patterns. This skill provides a curated reference for agent definitions, tools, callbacks, multi-agent orchestration, and graph workflows so you write correct code on the first try.
actual_capabilities: Define LlmAgent with model, instruction, tools, and output_schema
Create SequentialAgent, ParallelAgent, and LoopAgent orchestration patterns
Implement FunctionTool with ToolContext for state access and artifact storage
Set up callbacks for agent, model, and tool lifecycle hooks
Configure multi-agent delegation with sub_agents and AgentTool
Build graph-based Workflow API with nodes, edges, and conditional routing
limitations: Covers Python ADK SDK only; no Java or Go equivalents documented
Requires a scaffolded project before writing agent code
Graph-based Workflow API requires google-adk >= 2.0.0 and Python >= 3.11
Does not cover deployment workflows (use google-agents-cli-deploy skill instead)

use_cases.title: Build a single agent with tools and state
use_cases.description: Define an LlmAgent with custom FunctionTools, configure output schemas, and set up session state for conversational context.
use_cases.target_user: Python developers new to Google ADK

use_cases.title: Orchestrate multiple agents in a pipeline
use_cases.description: Use SequentialAgent, ParallelAgent, and LoopAgent to compose complex agent systems with data flowing through output keys.

use_cases.title: Create graph-based workflows with HITL
use_cases.description: Build ADK 2.0 Workflow graphs with conditional routing, fan-out/fan-in patterns, and human-in-the-loop approval gates.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers designing production agent pipelines

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic agent with a tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Use google-agents-cli-adk-code to help me create a new ADK agent called 'weather_helper' that uses Gemini and has a get_weather function tool.

prompt_templates.title: Add a custom tool with state access
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to write a FunctionTool that reads user_id from ToolContext state and queries a database, following the ADK patterns in google-agents-cli-adk-code.

prompt_templates.title: Build a sequential pipeline agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Using google-agents-cli-adk-code, guide me through creating a SequentialAgent that chains a summarizer and a question generator with output_key state propagation.

prompt_templates.title: Implement a graph workflow with HITL
prompt_templates.prompt: Reference google-agents-cli-adk-code to design an ADK 2.0 Workflow graph with a classification node, conditional routing, and a RequestInput node for human approval.

output_examples.input: I need to build an agent that searches a product database and returns structured results.
output: Step 1: Define a Pydantic model for the search result schema with fields like product_name, price, and availability.
Step 2: Create a FunctionTool with typed parameters (query: str, limit: int) that calls your database and returns a dict.
Step 3: Configure the Agent with model='gemini-flash-latest', output_schema=YourResultModel, and output_key='search_results' to persist results in session state.
Step 4: Run the agent with InMemoryRunner or deploy via the ADK CLI.

output_examples.input: How do I add human approval before a sensitive tool runs?
output: Option A: Use FunctionTool with require_confirmation=True for simple yes/no prompts.
Option B: Wrap with require_confirmation=callable for conditional logic based on tool arguments.
Option C: Enable app-level resumption with ResumabilityConfig(is_resumable=True) and use the request_input tool for multi-step clarification flows.
The user's reply is read from ctx.resume_inputs on the next invocation.

best_practices: Always define Pydantic output_schema for LLM agents in workflows to ensure JSON-serializable output for downstream nodes
Use factory functions for sub-agents (not module-level instances) to avoid 'agent already has a parent' errors during validation
Match the App(name=...) parameter to the agent directory name to prevent 'Session not found' errors during evaluation
anti_patterns: Importing load_web_page from google.adk.tools (module) instead of google.adk.tools.load_web_page (instance) — causes silent failures
Using module-level sub_agent instances instead of factory functions — triggers ValidationError on agent parent assignment
Mixing google_search grounding with FunctionTools — disables Automatic Function Calling for all tools in that agent

faq.question: What is Google ADK?
faq.answer: Google ADK (Agent Development Kit) is a framework for building LLM-powered agents in Python. It provides agent types, tools, callbacks, state management, and workflow orchestration.

faq.question: Do I need to scaffold a project before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill is for writing agent code inside an existing project. Run 'agents-cli scaffold create <name>' first, or use the google-agents-cli-scaffold skill.

faq.question: What Python version is required?
faq.answer: Google ADK core requires Python 3.10+. The graph-based Workflow API (ADK 2.0) requires Python 3.11 or later.

faq.question: Can I use non-Google models like Claude or GPT?
faq.answer: Yes. Use LiteLlm from google.adk.models.lite_llm to wrap any LiteLLM-supported provider, including Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, and local Ollama models.

faq.question: How do I handle human-in-the-loop interactions?
faq.answer: Enable ResumabilityConfig on your App, add the request_input tool to your agent, or use FunctionTool with require_confirmation=True. The user's reply becomes available on the next invocation via ctx.resume_inputs.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill contains only Markdown documentation files with API reference content. There are no scripts, no network calls, and no credential handling. Static analysis flagged markdown backticks and documentation URLs, but these are false positives.
